$comment: Canonical RE/PE → localAIStack trigger envelope. Emit one of these for every CES MergeOperation whose triggerConfig rule matches. The receiver (graphql_trigger_template.py / localAIStack /graphql) reads the top-level scalar fields for the existing updateProcessState mutation; the `outputVector`, `projection`, and `dispatch` blocks are the extension that carries CES-terminal-event semantics. Populate by mapping fields straight off the MergeOperation (src/engine/PerceptualSpaceSimulator.ts) and the machine's metadata.triggerConfig / metadata.mqttIntegration / governance / dispatchableAgent.
correlationId: <uuid v4 — shared across a causal cascade so the AI can stitch RE→AI→RE→AI chains>

source.$comment: Which engine emitted the event. RE = Reality Engine (TS, src/), PE = Perception Engine (RealityEngine_CPP). `endpoint` is the HTTPS base URL the dispatcher was watching.

ces.$comment: Mirrors MergeOperation fields one-for-one. machineCode = the AGX###/HHX### prefix from the JSON filename.

outputVector.$comment: The terminal event's payload. `values` is the raw bit-vector that just landed in perceptual space. `semantics[i].label` is the producer's name for cell i; this is the schema-on-write that lets the receiver decode the vector without consulting the machine JSON. `assertedLabel` is the one-hot category (or the join of multi-hot labels) — convenient for switch/case dispatch on the AI side.

projection.$comment: OPTIONAL. Populate when the producer's output region is intentionally overlaid onto another machine's input region (the AGX055 → AgYieldOptimizationAI pattern). `bitMapping[]` enumerates how each producer output cell lands on the target's input cells, AND what semantic schema the target should apply when interpreting them. `schemaOverlay` names the interpretation: the target's native inputSignalMap is bypassed in favour of the named overlay.

bitMapping.targetNativeSemantic: <what the target normally reads at this cell>
bitMapping.bridgeSemantic: <what THIS envelope means at this cell>

projection.schemaOverlay: <overlay name registered with the target AI agent>
projection.notes: Bridge machines re-purpose a shared perceptual region. The receiver MUST switch on schemaOverlay before interpreting the cells.

governance.$comment: Copied verbatim from PagingDecision (services/GovernanceResolver.ts:resolveForOutput). The RE side computes this; the AI side does not re-derive it.

dispatch.$comment: Which agent in localAIStack receives this envelope and what it should do. `agent` = the machine's metadata.dispatchableAgent. `action` = the matched rule's primary agentActions entry. `agentActionsCatalog` = the full machine.metadata.agentActions array so the AI can pick alternates. `trigger` = metadata.aiTrigger (the named hook the agent advertises). `endpoint.mutation` references the strawberry schema in localAIStack/services/api/routers/graphql_endpoint.py.

mqttContext.$comment: OPTIONAL. Populate for MQTT-driven machines (metadata.mqttIntegration present) so the AI can correlate the firing back to the raw broker payload. Omit when not applicable.
